regression_object: shallower range / higher seat behind you
progression_lever: reduce hand support, then deepen range, then slow the lowering to 3-4s
primary_benefit: lower-body strength and confident sit-to-stand mechanics
notes: Joint-sparing entry squat; keep knees tracking over toes and breathe out as you stand. Stop for sharp knee or hip pain.

progression_lever: slow 3-4s eccentric, then deepen range, then add a loaded backpack
primary_benefit: foundational lower-body strength and mobility
notes: Keep weight mid-foot and knees in line with toes; exhale on the way up. Ease range if knees complain.

progression_lever: add reps, then a heavier pack, then a slow lowering tempo
primary_benefit: leg and trunk strength with an upright, knee-friendly squat
notes: Intensity by feel (RPE), not assumed kilograms; brace and exhale on the rise. Stop for sharp joint pain.

regression_object: hold a chair or wall for balance
progression_lever: add range, then a backpack, then a slow eccentric
primary_benefit: single-leg strength and balance with low joint load
notes: Per-leg work that also trains balance; keep the front shin fairly vertical. Reduce depth for knee discomfort.

progression_lever: lower the seat, then remove hand support, then slow the descent
primary_benefit: advanced single-leg strength and fall-resilient mechanics
notes: Demanding; keep a chair or wall within reach. Stop for sharp knee pain and regress the seat height.

regression_object: hinge to a chair or wall behind you
progression_lever: increase range, then progress to a loaded RDL
primary_benefit: teaches a safe, neutral-spine hip hinge
notes: Movement-quality drill that protects the low back; keep the spine long, not rounded. Stop for back pain.

progression_lever: add range, then load with a backpack RDL
primary_benefit: hamstring and glute strength with hinge skill
notes: Hinge from the hips, not the waist; keep a long spine and breathe steadily. Stop for back pain.

progression_lever: add reps, then a heavier pack, then a 3-4s lowering
primary_benefit: posterior-chain (hamstring and glute) strength
notes: Gauge effort by RPE, not kilograms; keep the load close and the back flat. Stop for low-back pain.

regression_object: light fingertip support on a chair
progression_lever: remove support, then add range, then hold a light load
primary_benefit: single-leg hinge strength plus balance
notes: Combines posterior-chain strength with balance; keep hips square. Use support and stop for any back pain.

default_object: bath towel on a smooth floor
progression_lever: increase slide range, then slow the eccentric, then add single-leg
primary_benefit: eccentric hamstring strength and bridge control
notes: Strong eccentric load on the hamstrings; ease range to avoid cramping. Stop for sharp hamstring or back pain.

progression_lever: step the feet back, then progress to a countertop
primary_benefit: entry-level pressing strength for chest, shoulders, triceps
notes: Easiest push-up regression; keep a straight line from head to heels and breathe out on the press. Stop for shoulder pain.

regression_object: use a higher, stable surface (e.g., wall)
progression_lever: lower the surface height, then move to an incline chair
primary_benefit: mid-range pressing strength on the path to floor push-ups
notes: Use a counter that will not slide or tip; keep the core braced and the body in one line. Stop for shoulder pain.

progression_lever: lower the surface, then progress to knee push-ups
primary_benefit: near-floor pressing strength with manageable load
notes: Brace the chair so it cannot slip; keep a straight body line and steady breathing. Stop for shoulder pain.

progression_lever: increase range, then progress to a full push-up
primary_benefit: floor pressing strength with reduced load
notes: Keep hips from sagging by bracing the core; cushion the knees. Stop for shoulder or wrist pain.

progression_lever: add reps, then slow the lowering, then elevate the feet
primary_benefit: full-body pressing strength and trunk stability
notes: Keep a rigid line from head to heels and avoid flaring the elbows wide. Stop for shoulder pain and regress to knees.

progression_lever: increase hold time, then lean in farther
primary_benefit: isometric pressing endurance for shoulders and chest
notes: Keep breathing during the hold; isometrics can raise blood pressure, so avoid breath-holding. Stop for shoulder pain or dizziness.

progression_lever: increase hold time, then add bent-row reps with a load
primary_benefit: isometric back and grip strength
notes: Anchor must be secure; keep breathing and the neck relaxed. Stop for shoulder or neck pain.

progression_lever: heavier bottles, then a slow lowering, then more reps
primary_benefit: upper-back and biceps strength to balance pushing
notes: Hinge from the hips and keep the spine long; effort by RPE not kilograms. Stop for low-back pain.

progression_lever: heavier pack, then more reps, then a slow eccentric
primary_benefit: back and arm pulling strength with a heavier load
notes: Keep a flat back and neutral neck; gauge load by feel. Stop for low-back or shoulder pain.

regression_object: bend the knees / more upright body
progression_lever: straighten the body, then elevate the feet, then add reps
primary_benefit: advanced horizontal pulling strength for the whole back
notes: Test furniture stability and weight rating first; keep a rigid body and neutral neck. Stop for any joint pain.

progression_lever: add light cans, then more reps, then longer holds at the top
primary_benefit: mid-back, rear-shoulder and posture strength
notes: Lift with the back muscles, not the neck; keep the movements small. Stop for shoulder or neck pain.

progression_lever: heavier load, then longer carries, then a single-side carry
primary_benefit: grip, core and postural endurance plus light conditioning
notes: Keep the trunk tall and braced; effort by feel. Set the load down if posture fails. Stop for back pain.

progression_lever: heavier pack, then longer marches, then add a low step
primary_benefit: loaded postural endurance and light aerobic work
notes: Distribute the load on both shoulders and keep the spine tall. Stop for back pain or breathlessness.

default_object: one water bottle, can, or bag
progression_lever: heavier load, then longer carries, then a longer reach overhead
primary_benefit: anti-lean core strength and grip endurance
notes: The goal is to stay upright against the side load; keep shoulders level. Stop for back or shoulder pain.

progression_lever: heavier load, then faster controlled reps, then more range
primary_benefit: rotational core power and coordination
notes: Rotate through the trunk and hips, not the low back alone; keep it light. Stop for back pain.

default_object: bath towel + a secure side anchor
progression_lever: step farther from the anchor, then longer holds
primary_benefit: anti-rotation core stability that protects the spine
notes: The point is to NOT rotate; keep breathing and the spine neutral. Stop for back pain.

regression_object: two-hand support / lower the foot sooner
progression_lever: reduce hand support, then close the eyes briefly, then add a head turn
primary_benefit: single-leg balance to reduce fall risk
notes: Keep a chair within reach at all times; progress support gradually. Stop if you feel unsteady or dizzy.

progression_lever: narrow the base, then close the eyes briefly, then add arm movements
primary_benefit: static balance and ankle stability for fall prevention
notes: Practice near a counter you can grab; keep it safe. Stop if you feel unsteady or dizzy.

progression_lever: narrow the line, then add head turns, then carry a light load
primary_benefit: dynamic gait balance to reduce falls
notes: Keep a wall or counter within reach; go slowly. Stop if you feel unsteady or dizzy.

progression_lever: raise the step height, then add a backpack, then slow the lowering
primary_benefit: single-leg strength, balance and confident stair mechanics
notes: Use a stable step that will not move and hold a rail if needed; lead with the working leg. Stop for knee pain.

regression_object: smaller, slower steps / hold a counter
progression_lever: lift the knees higher, then quicken the pace, then add arm reaches
primary_benefit: low-impact aerobic conditioning and warm-up
notes: Keep it comfortable and rhythmic; hold a counter if balance is an issue. Stop for chest pain or dizziness.

progression_lever: quicken the pace, then add reps, then add a backpack
primary_benefit: aerobic conditioning plus repeated sit-to-stand power
notes: Keep knees tracking over toes and breathe through the reps. Stop for knee pain, chest pain, or dizziness.

progression_lever: quicken the pace, then lengthen the burst, then add a pack
primary_benefit: vigorous, time-efficient aerobic conditioning
notes: Use the handrail and a stable step; build pace gradually. Stop for chest pain, dizziness, or knee pain.

progression_lever: increase hold time, then progress to a full plank
primary_benefit: core and trunk stability with reduced load
notes: Keep breathing during the hold; do not let the low back sag. Stop for low-back or shoulder pain.

progression_lever: increase hold time, then elevate the feet, then add a reach
primary_benefit: whole-body trunk stability and endurance
notes: Keep breathing and a neutral spine; isometrics can raise blood pressure, so avoid breath-holding. Stop for back or shoulder pain.
